# evidence-gate — llms.txt
# AI-readable index of Evidence-Gate.
# Pre-publish source-verification rule library for HUMMBL governance content. Last updated: 2026-06-14.
#
# Query API: LLMs can parse this file to understand evidence verification rules
# Installation: git clone https://github.com/hummbl-dev/evidence-gate.git
# Documentation: https://github.com/hummbl-dev/evidence-gate
#
# evidence-gate categories:
#   HUMMBL:EVIDENCE Pre-publish source-verification
#   HUMMBL:GOVERNANCE Rule library for governance content
#   HUMMBL:VERIFICATION Source-verification rules

## Overview

Pre-publish source-verification rule library for HUMMBL governance content.

This repo is the **canonical home** for the Evidence-Gate v2 rule library consumed by `hummbl-production/scripts/case_study_verify.py` and other consumers. The canonical working tree lives on Anvil at `PROJECTS/evidence-gate/`; this repo mirrors to GitHub (`hummbl-dev/evidence-gate`) and Gitea (`HUMMBL/evidence-gate`).

## Layout

```
evidence-gate/
├── rules/                              # 8 ER-NNN.toml rules + _surfaces + _families
│   ├── _surfaces.toml                  # surface registry (external/internal/canonical-corpus globs)
│   ├── _families.toml                  # context-tag families (numeric, percent, broccolilly)
│   ├── ER-001-multiplier-no-source.toml
│   ├── ER-002-numeric-with-unit-no-source.toml
│   ├── ER-003-code-ident.toml
│   ...
├── scripts/                            # Rule management tooling
│   ├── validate.py                     # Validate all rules against schema
│   ├── lint.py                         # Lint rule format and conventions
│   └── test.py                         # Test rule matching logic
└── docs/                               # Rule documentation
    ├── RULE_SCHEMA.md                  # TOML schema for ER-NNN rules
    └── SURFACES.md                     # Surface registry documentation
```

## Usage

```bash
# Validate all rules
python scripts/validate.py

# Lint rule format
python scripts/lint.py

# Test rule matching
python scripts/test.py
```

## Rule Format

Each ER-NNN.toml rule includes:
- Rule ID and title
- Trigger pattern (regex or glob)
- Surface scope (external/internal/canonical-corpus)
- Context-tag family (numeric/percent/broccolilly)
- Verification action (block/warn/pass)
- Evidence requirements
